programmes.show_name: WWII in Colour: Road to Victory
programmes.show_description: The Liberation of Paris: In the summer of 1944, war-torn France reaches a crisis point. At its heart lies Paris - a symbol of liberty and democracy on the brink of collapse. (Part 6 of 10)

programmes.show_name: WWII in Colour: Road to Victory
programmes.show_description: Iwo Jima: When the US Air Force develops a new 'wonder' bomber they want a base close to Japan to launch their raids. Marines are tasked with taking the island of Iwo Jima. (Part 7 of 10)

programmes.show_name: WWII in Colour: Road to Victory
programmes.show_description: The Race for Berlin: Hitler's Nazi empire is in ruins, but there is still one city to take before the allies can declare victory - Berlin. (Part 8 of 10)

programmes.show_name: WWII in Colour: Road to Victory
programmes.show_description: Okinawa: As relentless firebombing and blockade of war-ravaged Japan shows no sign of securing a surrender, the US reluctantly prepares to invade. (Part 9 of 10)

programmes.show_name: WWII in Colour: Road to Victory
programmes.show_description: The Battle of the Philippine Sea: Two years after Japan's audacious attack on Pearl Harbor, America's great war machine now has them on the back foot. (Part 10 of 10)
